title: Rectal bleeding documentation
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

red_flags.text: Defines acute adult hospital lower gastrointestinal bleeding as bright or dark red blood per rectum, clots per rectum, or blood mixed with stool, while warning that brisk bleeding should not automatically be ascribed to haemorrhoids.
red_flags.rationale: The exact lower-GI bleeding section supports bleeding amount and character, stool association, onset, recurrence, associated symptoms, and presentation context without assigning source or severity.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Defines acute adult hospital lower gastrointestinal bleeding as bright or dark red blood per rectum, clots per rectum, or blood mixed with stool, while warning that brisk bleeding should not automatically be ascribed to haemorrhoids.

source.title: Diagnosis and management of acute lower gastrointestinal bleeding: guidelines from the British Society of Gastroenterology

exact_section.heading: Scope of the guideline and background presentation description

red_flags.text: Supports routine observations, full history and examination including digital rectal examination, appropriate blood tests, heart rate, systolic blood pressure, haemoglobin, and previous lower-GI-bleeding admission context.
red_flags.rationale: The exact initial-assessment section supports clinician-entered vital signs, general and abdominal assessment, and concern documentation without calculating a score or asserting stability.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports routine observations, full history and examination including digital rectal examination, appropriate blood tests, heart rate, systolic blood pressure, haemoglobin, and previous lower-GI-bleeding admission context.

source.title: Diagnosis and management of acute lower gastrointestinal bleeding: guidelines from the British Society of Gastroenterology

exact_section.heading: Management algorithm — initial assessment and Oakland variables

red_flags.text: Supports documenting whether anticoagulant or antiplatelet treatment is relevant to acute lower gastrointestinal bleeding; medicine interruption, reversal, and restart instructions are deliberately not mapped.
red_flags.rationale: The exact section supports recording anticoagulant or antiplatelet context and already reviewed results without changing medication.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports documenting whether anticoagulant or antiplatelet treatment is relevant to acute lower gastrointestinal bleeding; medicine interruption, reversal, and restart instructions are deliberately not mapped.

source.title: Diagnosis and management of acute lower gastrointestinal bleeding: guidelines from the British Society of Gastroenterology

exact_section.heading: Abstract and recommendations — anticoagulant and antiplatelet context

red_flags.text: Supports clinician assessment of haemodynamic stability, major versus minor bleeding context, and qualified clinician-decided hospital or outpatient follow-up documentation without calculating a score or inserting disposition advice.
red_flags.rationale: The exact stability and follow-up section supports clinician-entered disposition, safety net, and follow-up documentation without assigning urgency.
red_flags.evidence_extract: Supports clinician assessment of haemodynamic stability, major versus minor bleeding context, and qualified clinician-decided hospital or outpatient follow-up documentation without calculating a score or inserting disposition advice.

source.title: Diagnosis and management of acute lower gastrointestinal bleeding: guidelines from the British Society of Gastroenterology

source_limitations: Bleeding amount, haemodynamics, anaemia, anticoagulation, anorectal findings, consent, chaperone, source, diagnosis, tests, treatment, urgency, and disposition remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
All prior supported legacy-item mappings were removed because canonical item-level applicability and workflow-specific rationale were not present in explicit repository records.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
